items.title: Fake Police Officers Held French Couple at Knifepoint in $1M Bitcoin Robbery
items.summary: The incident is the latest in a spate of “$5 wrench attack” crypto kidnappings that have swept France in recent months.

items.title: CoinDesk 20 Performance Update: Stellar (XLM) Gains 5.1%, Leading Index Higher
items.summary: Near Protocol (NEAR), up 3.6% from Monday, was also a top performer.

items.title: Strategy records biggest STRC issuance day with estimated 1,420 BTC buy
items.summary: Strategy sold a record amount of STRC, estimated to fund 1,420 Bitcoin purchases in a single day after easing ATM sales restrictions.

items.title: Anthropic is suing the U.S. government for allegedly blacklisting its AI
items.summary: The maker of Claude filed suit against multiple federal agencies, arguing it was shut out of government procurement without the legal process required to ban a vendor.

items.title: Babylon-Ledger tie-up expands access to Bitcoin Vaults for collateral use
items.summary: The integration allows Ledger devices to sign BTCVault transactions as Babylon develops infrastructure to use Bitcoin as collateral.

items.title: Bitcoin’s quantum upgrade path: What BIP-360 changes and what it does not
items.summary: How BIP-360 reshapes Bitcoin’s quantum defense strategy, what it improves and why it stops short of full post-quantum security.

items.title: Morning Minute: Saylor Buys $1.28B in Bitcoin, Powered by STRC
items.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy just bought $1.28 billion worth of Bitcoin, while Nasdaq is teaming up with Kraken for tokenized stock trading.

items.title: French couple robbed of $1M in Bitcoin by criminals posing as police
items.summary: A French couple was forced to transfer about $1 million in Bitcoin during a fake police home invasion as wrench attacks keep rising, local media reported.

items.title: Slumping Strategy and Strive started with buy ratings at B. Riley
items.summary: The bank said the bitcoin treasury sector’s slump reset valuations, but opened the door for new digital credit financing models that could revive growth.

items.title: Bitcoin price analysis warns of potential dip after $72K liquidity sweep
items.summary: Bitcoin price taps $71,000, with liquidity thin above the spot price and heavy clusters below, setting up a potential sweep of the highs before another dip.

items.title: DeFi insurance is the final frontier of onchain finance
items.summary: DeFi lacks its final primitive. Insurance turns hidden risks into priced, programmable coverage. Programmable insurance with uncorrelated capital creates TVC safety nets.

items.title: South Korea sells $21.5M in recovered Bitcoin after custody breach
items.summary: Authorities sold the recovered Bitcoin in small batches over 11 days to avoid disrupting the market, according to local media reports.

items.title: U.S. requests October retrial for Tornado Cash developer Roman Storm
items.summary: Roman Storm, a Tornado Cash co-founder and developer, has criticized proceedings against him as an attempt “to make writing code a crime.”

items.title: UK Government’s Fraud Strategy Paints Crypto as ‘Growing Risk’
items.summary: Blockchain analysis firm Chainalysis argued that crypto’s transparency creates a “powerful flywheel” of fraud disruption.

items.title: US lawmakers probe Trump family-linked firm over Chinese stock scams
items.summary: Dominari Securities, tied to Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr., is among the brokerages named in the congressional probe.

items.title: A single crypto trader is sitting on a $194 million bet that bitcoin and ether will keep climbing
items.summary: Whales on Hyperliquid are piling into leveraged bitcoin and ether longs as BTC rallies to $71K, fueling bets the cryptocurrency will break above $75,000.

items.title: Traders snapped up nearly 600,000 BTC as bitcoin dipped below $70,000, blockchain data show
items.summary: Glassnode data shows strong demand during bitcoin’s recent correction, with 200,00 BTC purchased over the past two weeks.

items.title: Nvidia's Huang argues AI creates jobs, not destroys them, in rare official blog post
items.summary: The Nvidia CEO laid out a five-layer framework for AI infrastructure in his first standalone blog post in months, pushing back against the narrative that AI will destroy jobs.

items.title: Was Ethereum 'ultrasound money' a mistake? ETH down 65% vs. BTC since pivot
items.summary: Ethereum has failed to remain deflationary since the switch to Proof-of-Stake, as ETH's price has disappointed Ether investors, particularly against Bitcoin.

items.title: Hyperliquid's next upgrade to let seasoned traders take bigger bets with less capital
items.summary: Hyperliquid will introduce portfolio margin for real trading accounts, letting users offset risk across positions and support larger trades with less collateral.

items.title: Winklevoss Twins Move $130M in Bitcoin to Gemini Hot Wallets: Arkham
items.summary: Arkham claimed that the transfers were intended “presumably to sell,” though neither Cameron nor Tyler Winklevoss has commented on the move.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs to $71,000 as dollar, oil weaken after Trump comments on Iran war
items.summary: Crypto and risk assets rally as the dollar retreats following comments from Donald Trump suggesting the conflict in Iran may end soon, while bitcoin still faces a broader downtrend.

items.title: Tokenized stocks surpass $1B as Ondo, xStocks dominate sector
items.summary: Foresight Ventures says regulatory barriers and liquidity advantages are driving consolidation in tokenized equities as the market expands.

items.title: Strategy logs record STRC equity issuance on Monday, buys estimated 1,420 bitcoin
items.summary: The company amended its Omnibus Sales Agreement to allow multiple agents to execute sales of the same security outside regular trading hours.

items.title: Thailand crypto platforms freeze 10K accounts in AML crackdown: Report
items.summary: Thai crypto operators reportedly froze 10,000 suspected “mule accounts” as new AML checks and transfer delays took effect in a wider anti-fraud push.

items.title: US Bitcoin ETFs add $167M as altcoin funds extend outflows
items.summary: US Bitcoin ETFs added $167 million in inflows on Monday, while Ether, XRP and Solana funds saw three-day outflows despite a crypto market rebound.

items.title: Blackstone-owned AirTrunk secures record $1.24B loan for Tokyo AI data center
items.summary: A consortium of 12 banks, led by SMBC, MUFG, Crédit Agricole CIB and Société Générale, arranged the record financing for AirTrunk’s Tokyo data center expansion.

items.title: Hyperliquid’s tokenized futures hit $1.2B as traders bet on oil, stocks
items.summary: Trading has been driven largely by tokenized futures on equities and commodities such as oil, gold and silver.

items.title: Ether treasury firm Bitmine moves $19.5 million in ETH to Coinbase Prime
items.summary: The largest public ether holder sent two transfers totaling $19.5 million to Coinbase Prime hot wallets on Tuesday, though the moves don't necessarily signal selling.

items.title: Tron joins Agentic AI Foundation as founder sees future in AI
items.summary: Tron joined the Linux Foundation’s Agentic AI Foundation to help develop open infrastructure for AI agents alongside members including Circle and JPMorgan.

items.title: Pudgy Penguins launches its 'Club Penguin' moment, and the game doesn't feel like crypto at all
items.summary: Pudgy World went live with 12 towns, plot-based quests, and mini-games in what the team calls "one of the most technically advanced browser-based games ever created." The PENGU token jumped 9% on the news.

items.title: Wall Street Banks Weigh Lawsuit Over Crypto Banking Charters

items.summary: Several market participants, including MSTR and OTC traders, kept demand steady, helping BTC stay resilient.

items.summary: Bhutan has accumulated around 13,000 Bitcoin since launching state-backed mining operations in 2019, primarily fueled by hydroelectric energy.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin envisions ‘one-click’ Ether staking for institutions
